title: aquilegia pseudalpina f. maior
intro: Aquilegia pseudalpina f. maior, also known as the greater alpine columbine, is a flowering plant species within the aquilegia genus, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers in shades of pink to purple. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Aquilegia pseudalpina f. maior include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and protect against infections, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical treatments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
